name: AgentsRoom

runner.notes: Prepare a run worktree, open it in AgentsRoom, provide prompt.md exactly, then run harness verification.

inclusion: Direct ADE competitor focused on teams of coding agents.
pricing_model: Commercial desktop product; bring-your-own provider credentials where the underlying CLI requires them.
public_stack_signals: Official site positions AgentsRoom as a desktop app for macOS and Linux with an online demo using simulated data.
Runs multiple project folders and multiple CLI agent sessions in parallel.
Remote fleet and mobile companion features are presented as product features, including E2E relay for remote machines.
moat_hypotheses: rich communication modes for steering agents
setup_notes: Use the desktop app, not the simulated browser demo, for any scored benchmark run.

sources.notes: Product positioning, supported agents, feature surface, role agents, remote fleet, review, and mobile companion claims.

research.positioning: Visual desktop cockpit for coordinating many coding agents across projects, providers, roles, and machines.

backend: remote fleet relay according to official site
packaging: desktop app for macOS and Linux identified on official site
unknowns: Internal desktop framework is not published in official sources reviewed.
Windows support and mobile app internals need direct install verification.
Persistence, sync protocol, and relay implementation are not public in official sources reviewed.

infrastructure: Desktop app opens real project folders on the user's computer.
Agents run as provider CLI sessions in terminals managed by the app.
Remote fleet feature connects multiple machines through an E2E relay according to the official site.
Mobile companion can send prompts and screenshots to the active desktop agent according to the official site.

orchestration: role-labeled agents such as DevOps, Frontend, Backend, Architect, QA, Fullstack, Mobile, Security, PM, Marketing, Git Expert, SEO, localization, and brainstorming
agent teams that hand work between roles
workspace_isolation: project folders are real local folders
agents are attached to projects and roles
diff review can be filtered by agent
remote_mobile: drawing, screenshot sharing, browser pointer, prompt library, message queue

install_surface: open prepared benchmark worktree as a project folder
parity_risks: The online demo is simulated and must not be used for scoring.
Broad remote/mobile feature set is outside the current v0.1 local task suite.
Closed-source internals limit stack comparison to public claims and hands-on behavior.

hypotheses: Strong visual cockpit may reduce operator overload when many agents run at once.
Remote fleet plus mobile companion creates a broader control surface than local-only ADEs.
Role library and scheduled tasks make the product feel like a managed agent team rather than a terminal wrapper.
evidence: Official site describes one screen for multiple projects, agents, providers, and statuses.
Official site describes backlog, agent teams, scheduled tasks, remote fleet, mobile companion, review by agent, and commit context.
unknowns: Quality of local isolation, reproducibility, and verifier-proof evidence needs a hands-on run.
